topic.name: Historical Figures and Their Impact
topic.description: This topic covers notable historical individuals and their contributions or significance in various contexts, including military service, social activism, and intellectual achievements.

Document 1: Virgil Brennan was an Australian aviator and flying ace of the Second World War.
Brennan was officially credited with the destruction of 10 Axis aircraft.
Document 2: Modesta Ávila was a protestor in Orange County, California who became the county's first convicted felon.
Avila is considered to be a folk heroine of Latino people of the county.
Document 3: Al-Risalah al-Dhahabiah is a medical dissertation on health and remedies attributed to Ali ibn Musa al-Ridha.
The treatise is revered as the most precious Islamic literature in the science of medicine.

topic.name: Royal Navy Warships
topic.description: The documents discuss various classes of warships built for the Royal Navy and their specifications, histories, and roles.

Document 1: Hector-class ironclads were a pair of armoured frigates built for the Royal Navy in the 1860s.
Both ships were assigned to the Reserve Fleet from 1868 until they were paid off in 1885 – 86.
Document 2: HMS Brazen was a B-class destroyer built for the Royal Navy around 1930.
Brazen later began escorting coastal convoys in the English Channel and was sunk in late July 1940 by German aircraft whilst doing so.

topic.name: Historical Communities and Figures
topic.description: This cluster discusses historical communities and notable figures, highlighting their contributions and significance over time.

Document 1: Huletts Landing is a hamlet in the town of Dresden in northern Washington County, New York, United States.
The hamlet derives its name from the Hulett family, which resided in the area for several generations.
Document 2: Beatriz Michelena was a Venezuelan American actress during the silent film era, known at the time for her operatic soprano voice.
She was one of the few Latina stars visible on the silver screen in the United States in the 1910s.
Document 3: The DeKalb County Courthouse is located in the county seat of DeKalb County, Illinois, U.S.A.
The current courthouse was constructed in 1905 amid controversy over where the courthouse and thus, ultimately, the county seat would be located.

topic.name: Atlantic and Indian Ocean Hurricanes
topic.description: The documents discuss various hurricane seasons and specific storms from the Atlantic and Indian Ocean, highlighting their formation, impact, and historical significance.

Document 1: The 1869 Atlantic hurricane season was the earliest season in the Atlantic hurricane database in which there were at least ten tropical cyclones.
The strongest hurricane was a Category 3 on the modern-day Saffir-Simpson Hurricane Scale which struck New England at that intensity.
Document 2: Hurricane Alberto caused the worst flooding in western Cuba in 32 years.
Alberto produced heavy rainfall in western Cuba, causing flash flooding and severe damage.
Document 3: The 2000-01 South-West Indian Ocean cyclone season was fairly quiet with only five named storms.
Ando would become the most intense cyclone of the year, reaching peak winds of 195 km/h (120 mph) according to the Météo-France office.

topic.description: A collection of summaries and analyses of various episodes from the American science fiction television series The X-Files.

topic.description: The documents discuss various naval battles and actions involving British and French forces during the late 18th century, highlighting the conflicts arising from colonial rivalries and wars.

topic.name: Byzantine-Arab Conflicts
topic.description: The documents discuss various military engagements and sieges between the Byzantine Empire and Arab forces during the early medieval period.

Document 1: The Siege of Nicaea of 727 was an unsuccessful attempt by the Umayyad Caliphate to capture the Byzantine city of Nicaea.

Document 2: The Battle of Lalakaon was fought in 863 between the Byzantine Empire and an invading Arab army.
The Byzantine victories proved decisive: the main threats to the Byzantine borderlands were eliminated.
Document 3: The Battle of Anzen was fought on 22 July 838 between the Byzantine Empire and the forces of the Abbasid Caliphate.
The defeat opened the way for the brutal sack of Amorion.
